name: Cloutie Dumpling with Ginger Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Sieve the flour and mix it with the breadcrumbs, brown sugar, and spices.
2. Melt the butter or vegetable fat gently with the treacle. Mix the breadcrumbs, currants, and sultanas. Beat the egg and milk together and add to the dry ingredients, along with the grated carrot and apple. Use your hands; add more milk if necessary to give a soft mixture that drops easily from the spoon.
3. Place a square of cotton sheet in the pot of boiling water with an upturned plate on the bottom. Remove the scalded cloth, sprinkle it with flour, and put in the dumpling. Draw up the edges and tie firmly with white string, leaving enough room for the pudding to expand.
4. Lower the dumpling back into the boiling pan. Keep water topped up. Bring back to a boil. Boil steadily but gently for 4 hours (it can be longer but shouldn't be less).
5. Remove the dumpling and dip it straight in and out of cold water. Unwrap the dumpling on a serving plate.
6. The skin will initially be white from the flour. Place the dumpling on its plate in a very low oven to dry off for 20 minutes, allowing it to develop a fine, dark glossy skin.
7. Meanwhile, make the ginger cream by beating the wine with the egg yolks over hot water until the mixture is thick, white, and fluffy. Stir in the single cream.
Serve the ginger cream in a pretty glass jug with the hot pudding. The dumpling cuts wonderfully rich and dark. Leftover slices are delicious fried in butter—lovely with cream for a special tea-time treat.
